# Plag Mincha

[Back to all zmanim](index.md)

## Plag Ahavat Shalom

Plag hamincha, described here as the earliest time that Shabbos can be started, according to Rabbi Yaakov Moshe Hillel as published in the luach of the Bais Horaah of Yeshivat Chevrat Ahavat Shalom.

It is calculated as 1.25 shaos zmaniyos before tzais at 3.8°, with the shaos zmaniyos based on a day that starts at alos 16.1° and ends at tzais 3.8°.

This time may not be available in northern or southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle and north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not reach low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getPlagAhavatShalom`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getPlagAhavatShalom`

## Plag Alos 16 Point 1 To Tzais Geonim 7 Point 083 Degrees

Plag hamincha according to the opinion that the day starts at alos 16.1° before sunrise and ends at tzais 7.083° after sunset.

It is calculated as 10.75 shaos zmaniyos after alos 16.1°, with the shaos zmaniyos based on the day from alos 16.1° to tzais 7.083°.

This time may not be available in northern or southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle and north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not reach low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getPlagAlos16Point1ToTzaisGeonim7Point083Degrees`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getPlagAlos16Point1ToTzaisGeonim7Point083Degrees`

## Plag Alos To Sunset

Deprecated because it should be used lechumra only. It gives a very late time, often after shkiah, and using it lekula can result in chillul Shabbos and similar concerns.

Plag hamincha based on the opinion that the day starts at alos 16.1° and ends at sea level sunset.

It is calculated as 10.75 shaos zmaniyos after alos 16.1°, with the shaos zmaniyos based on the day from alos 16.1° before sunrise to sea level sunset.

This calculation can place plag after sunset, so it should be used lechumra only.

This time may not be available in northern or southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle and north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not reach low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getPlagAlosToSunset`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getPlagAlosToSunset`

## Plag Hamincha 120 Minutes

Deprecated because it should be used lechumra only. It gives a very late time, often after shkiah, and using it lekula can result in chillul Shabbos and similar concerns.

Plag hamincha according to the Magen Avraham, with the day starting at alos 120 minutes. It should be used lechumra only.

It is calculated as 10.75 shaos zmaniyos after alos 120 minutes.

Because this zman is based on an extremely early alos and a very late tzais, it gives a very late time, often after shkiah, and can result in chillul Shabbos and similar concerns if not treated as lechumra.

This time may not be available in the Arctic Circle, where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getPlagHamincha120Minutes`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getPlagHamincha120Minutes`

## Plag Hamincha 120 Minutes Zmanis

Deprecated because it should be used lechumra only. It gives a very late time, often after shkiah, and using it lekula can result in chillul Shabbos and similar concerns.

Plag hamincha based on sunrise being 120 minutes zmaniyos, or one-sixth of the day, after dawn. It should be used lechumra only.

It is calculated as 10.75 shaos zmaniyos after dawn, using the shaah zmanis for the 120-minutes-zmanis calculation.

Because this zman is based on an extremely early alos and a very late tzais, it should be used lechumra only.

This time may not be available in the Arctic Circle, where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getPlagHamincha120MinutesZmanis`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getPlagHamincha120MinutesZmanis`

## Plag Hamincha 26 Degrees

Deprecated because it should be used lechumra only. It produces a very late time, often after shkiah, and using it lekula can result in chillul Shabbos and similar concerns.

Plag hamincha based on a day that starts at alos when the sun is 26° below the horizon and ends at tzais when the sun is 26° below the horizon. This zman is intended for lechumra only.

Calculated as 10.75 shaos zmaniyos after dawn, using shaos zmaniyos based on the 26° alos-to-26° tzais day.

This calculation uses an extremely early alos and a very late tzais, so it can produce a very late plag hamincha.

The zman may not be available in northern or southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle and north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not get low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getPlagHamincha26Degrees`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getPlagHamincha26Degrees`

## Plag Hamincha 60 Minutes

Plag hamincha according to the Magen Avraham, with the day starting 60 minutes before sunrise and ending 60 minutes after sunset.

Calculated as 10.75 shaos zmaniyos after dawn, using shaos zmaniyos based on a day from 60 minutes before sunrise to 60 minutes after sunset.

The zman may not be available in places such as the Arctic Circle, where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one day when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getPlagHamincha60Minutes`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getPlagHamincha60Minutes`

## Plag Hamincha 72 Minutes

Deprecated because it should be used lechumra only. It produces a very late time, often after shkiah, and using it lekula can result in chillul Shabbos and similar concerns.

Plag hamincha according to the Magen Avraham, with the day starting 72 minutes before sunrise and ending 72 minutes after sunset. This zman is intended for lechumra only.

Calculated as 10.75 shaos zmaniyos after dawn, using shaos zmaniyos based on a day from 72 minutes before sunrise to 72 minutes after sunset.

By this calculation, plag hamincha can occur after sunset, so it should only be used lechumra.

The zman may not be available in places such as the Arctic Circle, where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one day when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getPlagHamincha72Minutes`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getPlagHamincha72Minutes`

Plag hamincha using a 72-minute zmanis dawn. This zman is intended for lechumra only.

Calculated as 10.75 shaos zmaniyos after dawn, using shaos zmaniyos for the 72-minute zmanis calculation.

By this calculation, plag hamincha can occur after sunset, so it should only be used lechumra.

The zman may not be available in places such as the Arctic Circle, where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one day when it does not set.

## Plag Hamincha Ateret Torah

Plag hamincha according to Chacham Yosef Harari-Raful of Yeshivat Ateret Torah.

The day starts at alos, defined as 1/10 of the day before sunrise, and usually ends 40 minutes after sunset. Shaos zmaniyos are based on that day, and plag hamincha is 10.75 shaos zmaniyos after alos.

The end of the day is configurable to an offset other than 40 minutes after sunset.

This time may not be available when the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getPlagHaminchaAteretTorah`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getPlagHaminchaAteretTorah`

## Plag Hamincha Baal Hatanya

Plag hamincha according to the Baal Hatanya, where the day is calculated from sunrise to sunset.

It is calculated as 10.75 shaos zmaniyos after netz amiti, sunrise. More details are available at [About Our Zmanim Calculations @ Chabad.org](https://www.chabad.org/library/article_cdo/aid/3209349/jewish/About-Our-Zmanim-Calculations.htm).

This time may not be available when the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getPlagHaminchaBaalHatanya`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getPlagHaminchaBaalHatanya`

## Plag Hamincha GRAFixed Local Chatzos To Sunset

Plag hamincha according to [Rav Moshe Feinstein’s](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Moshe_Feinstein) opinion, calculated according to the [GRA](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vilna_Gaon).

The day ends at sunset, and plag hamincha is 4.75 shaos zmaniyos after fixed local chatzos.

This time may not be available when the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getPlagHaminchaGRAFixedLocalChatzosToSunset`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getPlagHaminchaGRAFixedLocalChatzosToSunset`

## Plag Hamincha GRA

Plag hamincha according to the [GRA](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vilna_Gaon). Plag hamincha is the earliest time that Shabbos can be started.

It is calculated as 10.75 shaos zmaniyos after sunrise. The day is calculated from sunrise to sunset, with the exact sunrise and sunset depending on whether elevation is used.

This time may not be available when the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ZmanimCalendar.getPlagHaminchaGRA`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ZmanimCalendar.getPlagHaminchaGRA`
